rulings.Case Title: VINOD KRIPALANI et al VS. PAUL MARTIN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause Why Judgment Debtors Paul Martin And Sharon Yost, Jointly And Severally, Should Not Be Held In Contempt
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, March 06, 2014, line 2. Order To Show Cause Why Judgment Debtors Paul Martin And Sharon Yost, Jointly And Severally, Should Not Be Held In Contempt. Continued to April 18, 2014. Parties are to meet and confer to chose 3 contractors, one of which should be Spryscapes (if willing and available) to provide estimates regarding wall correction in compliance with the arbitration award/judgment and to determine whether correction is possible without damage to or rebuilding of the Plainitff's wall. If parties cannot agree, then each party shall designate one contractor in addition to Spryscapes (if Spryscapes is not available defendants shall designate two). Both Plaintiffs and Defendants must provide access to the chosen contractors on a 24 hour written notice during the week of March 24, 2014 for the purposes outlined above. Contractors must be chosen no later than March 17, 2014. Parties shall file joint or separate supplemental briefing not to exceed 3 pages no later than March 20, 2014 providing the Court with the list of contractors and tentative dates for estimates. Parties shall file further supplemental briefing no to exceed 5 pages excluding exhibits no later than April 11, 2014 providing the Court with information regarding estimates and availabilities of the chosen contractors to perform the work during the months of May through July 2014. = (501/LKS) LATE TENTATIVE

rulings.Case Title: GARY STRENG VS. KEYNOTE PROPERTIES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Alternative Summary Adjudication Of Alvin H. Baum, Jr As An Individual, And As Trustee For The Alvin H. Baum, Jr. Trust
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calekdar for Thursday, March 06, 2014 line 4. DEFENDANT ALVIN BAUM's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT or in the Alternative Summary Adjudication Of Alvin H. Baum, Jr As An Individual, And As Trustee For The Alvin H. Baum, Jr. Trust. DENIED. Traible issues of fact exists as to (1) whether Streng was Dowdee's subtenant (Streng Depo. 49:10-13; Streng Declaration paragraph 5); and (2) whether Streng's subtenancy was approved (Streng Declaration paragraph 6-7; Williams Depo. 43:22-44:8). Plaintiff provided sufficient evidence surrounding the lockout events (Streng' s Declaration paragraphs 10-29) so that defendant's conduct can reasonably be regarded as "outrageous" for the purposes of intentional infliction of emotional distress cause of action. = (501/LKS) LATE TENTATIVE

rulings.Case Title: DEAN WOO VS. GENIE NICHOLAS et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, March 6, 2014, line 12. DEFENDANT GENIE NICHOLAS's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. DENIED. Moving party cites no authority for the proposition that complaint must be verified by original signature (and not by fax as was done in this case). Additionally, Defendant's motion is untimely per CCP 1005. To the extent Defendant moves to quash service of summons and complaint (Moving papers 3:9-10), the motion to quash is denied. Defendant does not provide a declaration to support lack of proper service. "Post and Mail" application granted on February 3, 2014. Defendant's counsel is ordered to comply with SFLR 2.6B and C in future. = (501/LKS). LATE TENTATIVE.
